description: Parametric dimensions for rectangular tables including dining, meeting, and side tables. Does NOT cover coffee tables (use coffee_table skill instead).

dining_table.description: Standard dining or kitchen table used with chairs at seated height

width_cm.description: Long horizontal dimension (X-axis). Always >= depth_cm. Common sizes: 120, 140, 160, 180, 200, 240 cm.

depth_cm.description: Short horizontal dimension (Y-axis). Standard dining comfort requires minimum 75 cm. Typical range 80-95 cm.

height_cm.description: Floor to top surface. Standard dining height 74-76 cm. Ergonomic range 72-78 cm for use with standard chairs.

leg_thickness_cm.description: Square or round leg cross-section. Slender contemporary legs 4-5 cm. Traditional/farmhouse legs 7-8 cm. Hairpin rod legs 1-2 cm (override cap).

top_thickness_cm.description: Tabletop slab thickness. Marble/stone: 3-4 cm. Timber: 3-4 cm. Sintered stone: 1.2-2 cm. Glass: 1-1.5 cm.

meeting_conference_table.description: Large rectangular table for office or boardroom use

width_cm.description: Long dimension of conference table. Common: 180, 200, 240, 300, 360, 400 cm.

depth_cm.description: Short dimension. Conference tables are typically wider (deeper) than dining tables to accommodate laptops and documents.

height_cm.description: Standard office desk/table height. ADA compliant range 71.1-86.4 cm.

leg_thickness_cm.description: Larger section legs or trestle/beam structures for long tables.

top_thickness_cm.description: Conference tables often have thicker tops for rigidity over long spans.

coffee_table.description: Low rectangular table for living rooms. NOTE: route to build_coffee_table_model() not build_rectangular_table_model()

width_cm.description: Long dimension of coffee table top.

depth_cm.description: Short dimension of coffee table top. Typically 60-70% of sofa depth.

height_cm.description: Low table height. Should be approximately equal to sofa seat height (40-45 cm) for comfortable reach.

leg_thickness_cm.description: Coffee tables have slender legs. Very thin metal legs: 1-2 cm. Standard: 3-4 cm.

sanity_checks.rule: Width must be greater than or equal to depth
sanity_checks.message: If width_cm < depth_cm, swap the two values — longer side is always width.

sanity_checks.rule: Dining table height must be in ergonomic range
sanity_checks.message: Dining table height outside 72-78 cm range. Verify measurement. Standard is 74-76 cm.

sanity_checks.rule: Coffee table height must be low
sanity_checks.message: Coffee table height outside 35-52 cm range. If height > 60 cm this is likely a side/console table.

sanity_checks.rule: Leg thickness must not exceed 10 cm
sanity_checks.message: Leg thickness > 10 cm is unrealistic for a table leg. Value may represent apron depth instead. Cap at 10 cm.

sanity_checks.rule: Leg height must be positive after subtracting top thickness
sanity_checks.message: height_cm - top_thickness_cm < 20 cm leaves almost no room for legs. Check height extraction.

sanity_checks.rule: Width must not exceed plausible single-piece top span
sanity_checks.message: Width > 500 cm is outside any realistic single-table scenario. Check unit (mm vs cm confusion?).

sanity_checks.rule: Depth must be within realistic range
sanity_checks.message: Depth outside 40-130 cm range is unusual. Verify measurement source.

avoid_for: very narrow console (depth < 45 cm) -> use build_console_table_model()
